mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or HOUST fails to exceed the 6-month moving average in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 12-month moving average signals a weakening manufacturing sector that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening manufacturing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to decline below the 12-month moving average in 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that boosts unemployment benefits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or UNRATE fails to exceed 4.5% in 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 1.5% signals a easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening economy that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening economy periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or GDPC1 fails to exceed the 6-month moving average in 65% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 12-month moving average signals a weakening manufacturing sector that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening manufacturing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to decline below the 12-month moving average in 75% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that boosts unemployment benefits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or UNRATE fails to exceed 4.5% in 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) above the 6-month moving average and a 2-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening housing market and a strengthening economy that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening housing and economy periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or HOUST fails to exceed the 6-month moving average in 60% of instances or GDPC1 fails to exceed the 6-month moving average in 65% of instances.
